pi_items.source: Verified from setup_task.sh logic for removing old case directories and export_result.sh glob pattern /home/ga/Cases/Fragment_Recovery_2024*/autopsy.db.
Verified from task.json description as a requested parameter for the forensic case creation. Unchecked by the automated scripts.
Verified from setup_task.sh which dynamically creates this FAT32 disk image containing the scattered fragments.
Verified from setup_task.sh which explicitly creates this directory for the user to export fragments into.
Verified from export_result.sh and verifier.py which hash the file at this exact location to confirm reconstruction.
Verified from export_result.sh which reads this exact file path to validate its content strings.
Verified from export_result.sh which outputs the evaluation JSON to this file path, consumed by verifier.py.
Verified from setup_task.sh logic mapping part_a, part_b, and part_c to swap_frag.sys, sys_cache.bin, and win_temp.dat respectively. The correct sequence is matched in verifier.py using regex.

privileged_info_summary: The task revolves around a synthetically generated FAT32 forensic disk image containing a split JPEG file. Analysis of the setup scripts confirms that the source image is divided into three parts: part_a (first third), part_b (middle third), and part_c (final third). These parts are explicitly named and placed as swap_frag.sys, sys_cache.bin, and win_temp.dat respectively, verifying the correct sequential order required for reconstruction. The expected case name, disk image location, and reporting file paths exactly match the task constraints and evaluation scripts.
